summary: Re-evaluate H1B-only PASSes under looser rule + broader round-2 discovery → master dashboard

rescued.title: Specialist, Learning and Development Operations
rescued.h1b: Nike is a confirmed H1B sponsor overall (1,779+ LCA filings, including HR Strategic Planning Director, HRBP, and Lead L&D Advisor in recent cycles). People/L&D non-tech filings are sparse relative to tech volume, but non-zero. Under the updated looser rule this is a warning + small penalty (-3), not a hard PASS.
rescued.apply_mechanism: Nike Careers portal (Workday) — apply directly at the URL above
rescued.reason: Strong P1b (Learning Program Operations) fit. Role requires end-to-end learning logistics, vendor coordination, budget tracking, stakeholder communication, and program calendar management — all ★★★ strengths for Jamie. 2+ YOE requirement met (~3 years). On-site Beaverton is Tier 1 Portland local with geographic advantage. MS Applied Org Psych exceeds the education bar. Only gap is Workday Learning proficiency (LMS administration), which is learnable and not the primary function. H1B: Nike sponsors overall but People/L&D LCAs are sparse — surface warning but do not block. Posting is ~4 months old but confirmed active via aggregator mirror. Score: 82 base + 5 Portland + 3 P1b - 3 H1B warning = 87.

stretch.apply: Online application at jobs.oregonstate.edu/postings/180935 — requires resume, cover letter explaining qualifications, and three professional references. Contact: Nicole Kent (nicole.kent@oregonstate.edu).

stretch.title: Entry Level Management Consulting - Talent, Change & Behavior
stretch.h1b: PwC filed 653 H1B LCAs for the "ASSOCIATE" title (2022-2025), split between PwC Advisory Services LLC (409 records, median $95K) and PwC LLP (244 records, median $79K). No filings found under "talent" or "human resources" keyword searches, but Big4 firms file by generic title — "ASSOCIATE" is the standard title for all entry-level consulting tracks including Talent/Change. Advisory Services LLC is the consulting entity where this role sits. H1B sponsorship is likely but not SOC-code-confirmed for People-side roles; treated as a WARNING + partial credit (+5 not +10).
stretch.apply: PwC careers portal — this is a talent pool page, not a single requisition. Jamie applies to a general Management Consulting pool and gets aligned to Talent/Change/Behavior based on her background. Submit via the job alert/apply form on the page.

all_round2.company: LinkedIn Search: People Program Manager Portland
all_round2.title: People Program Manager (LinkedIn Search — Portland, OR)
all_round2.h1b: Unknown ⚠️
all_round2.location: Portland, OR (search results page — no specific posting)
all_round2.reason: The provided URL is a LinkedIn job search results page, not a specific job posting. Extensive cross-search across Indeed, Glassdoor, LinkedIn, Greenhouse, Lever, ZipRecruiter, and company career pages found ZERO currently-live postings matching 'People Program Manager' in Portland metro as of June 11, 2026. The only confirmed posting with this exact title (Mercury fintech) was removed December 18, 2024. Other roles surfaced: Nike Coordinator TM (stale cached post, Lensa 402), Nike T&T Engagement Manager (4-month contract, no H1B), ModusLink PM AI Enablement (zero H1B filings, remote = hard PASS), College Possible Program Manager (zero H1B filings, AmeriCorps-adjacent nonprofit). This is a thin-market keyword — recommend broadening search to adjacent P1/P1b titles: 'Talent Development Specialist/Manager,' 'Employee Experience Program Manager,' 'People Operations Specialist,' 'Learning & Development Program Manager,' or 'Organizational Effectiveness Specialist' in Portland/Beaverton. No specific role to evaluate; verdict is PASS on this search URL as-is.
all_round2.apply_mechanism: N/A — search results page, no single application target

all_round2.title: Talent Mobility Coordinator
all_round2.h1b: WARNING — Nike filed 2,037+ H1B LCAs in 2025 but People/HR non-tech filings are exclusively manager/lead/director level (HR REPORTING ANALYST, HRBP, LEAD L&D ADVISOR, MANAGER HR OPERATIONS REPORTING). Zero coordinator-level People/HR/Talent filings found in 2024 or 2025. Under the updated looser H1B rule this is a warning + penalty rather than hard PASS, but the pattern strongly indicates coordinator-level Talent roles are not sponsored at Nike. No explicit "no sponsorship" JD language found (JD inaccessible).
all_round2.location: Beaverton, OR (Nike World HQ — Tier 1 local)
all_round2.apply_mechanism: Employee referral portal only — redirects to internal Nike HR portal (authentication required); not accessible via public careers.nike.com or LinkedIn. Posting not confirmed live.
all_round2.reason: PASS on two compounding grounds: (1) Posting is not live — the employee referral URL redirects to an authenticated internal Nike HR portal, LinkedIn search returns no results for this role in Beaverton, and Nike's public careers page shows only 2 HR roles currently open (neither is this). Cannot verify JD, requirements, or sponsorship language. (2) H1B pattern at coordinator level is effectively non-existent — Nike's 2,037 LCA filings in 2025 include only manager/lead/director-level People roles; zero coordinator-level People/Talent/HR filings found across 2024 or 2025. Even under the looser H1B-as-warning rule, a pattern of zero filings at the target grade (coordinator) combined with a dead/inaccessible posting does not clear the bar for a STRETCH. Role scope (Talent Mobility, Tier 1 Beaverton location) is genuinely P1 and would be a strong fit if sponsorship were confirmed — flag for re-evaluation if Nike posts a new version with verified sponsorship language.

all_round2.title: Learning Program Manager
all_round2.h1b: Confirmed — 12 total LCAs, 11 non-tech (Operations Manager, Student Operations Manager, GTM Manager, Engagement Manager). Verified sponsor for non-tech roles.
all_round2.reason: DEAD POSTING — removed from Built In on October 2, 2025 (8+ months ago). Not on Stepful's current careers page. The Wellfound URL is surfacing a stale, closed listing. Even if the role were live: (1) NYC hybrid is Tier 3 relocation requiring 85%+ honest match; (2) instructional design is listed as a required qualification — Jamie's ★☆☆ there triggers Guardrail 4 and caps the score; (3) the role leans heavily toward curriculum content oversight and team-leading curriculum developers, which is closer to Instructional Design Manager than Learning Program Manager in practice. Do not apply.

all_round2.title: Manager, Program Manager (Enterprise Learning & Skills)
all_round2.h1b: Pearson Education H1B filings 2023-2025 are exclusively tech (software engineers, cloud architects, data engineers) and healthcare roles. Zero non-tech/People/L&D/Program Management LCA filings found across all three years. H1B sponsorship for this type of role is unconfirmed — would be a warning/penalty under updated rules.
all_round2.reason: Posting is EXPIRED — Pearson.jobs shows "Program Manager has expired." confirmed via live browser render. Dead posting is a hard disqualifier regardless of fit. Score set to 0. Do not apply.

all_round2.title: Learning and Development Manager
all_round2.h1b: COLAS USA has zero H1B LCA filings in h1bdata.info across all years and all COLAS entities (COLAS USA, COLAS INC, COLASISS INC, COLAS ISS INC). All 28 total COLAS-family filings are tech/ERP/finance roles. No People, HR, Training, or L&D filings anywhere in the database. Per updated H1B rule this is a WARNING (not hard PASS alone), but combined with no explicit sponsorship language in the JD and zero organizational history of People-role sponsorship, the practical sponsorship probability is very low.
all_round2.reason: HARD PASS on three independent grounds: (1) TITLE DISQUALIFIER — 'Manager' is an explicit hard-reject title per Jamie's profile rules; exceptions apply only to consulting Assoc/Analyst levels. (2) EXPERIENCE GAP — role requires 5+ years; Jamie has ~3 years, a 2+ year gap that caps the score at 60 regardless of fit. (3) PRIMARY SKILLS MISMATCH — role centers on instructional design methodology (ADDIE/SAM), eLearning authoring, and LMS/TMS administration — all ★☆☆ for Jamie, and two ★☆☆ primary requirements auto-cap the score at 60. Additional concerns: posting appears dead (SimplyHired shows 'temporarily unavailable,' original edtech.com URL returned 504), zero COLAS People/HR H1B filing history, role requires managing a team (no people-management experience in Jamie's background), and construction/transportation domain knowledge is preferred. Even setting aside the title disqualifier, score lands ~42 — well below the 70 STRETCH threshold.

all_round2.title: Senior Learning Consultant II
all_round2.h1b: Instructure has only 5 H1B filings (all 2025), all in tech/engineering/product roles — zero People, L&D, or consulting non-tech filings. Under the updated warning rule this is a flag, not an automatic hard PASS; however it is moot given the other disqualifiers.
all_round2.location: US-REMOTE (New York) — but NOTE: posting explicitly requires on-site in upper Manhattan 5 days/week per SOW. Effectively a NYC on-site role, not remote.
all_round2.reason: Multiple hard disqualifiers stack: (1) "Senior" title triggers instant PASS per Jamie's rules — this is a 5+ year Senior Consultant role, not a Senior Associate exception. (2) Primary requirement is minimum 5 years of Instructure/Canvas LMS product experience — Jamie has zero Canvas experience; this is a ★☆☆ skill required as the core function. (3) Requires minimum 3 years of classroom teaching experience — Jamie has none. (4) Role is on-site 5 days/week in upper Manhattan per client SOW — not a remote role despite the listing label; Jamie is in Portland. (5) Scope is Canvas LMS implementation/adoption consulting, which falls under the LMS-platform-specific roles Jamie avoids (equivalent to Workday/SAP implementation). (6) Zero non-tech H1B filing history at Instructure adds further H1B risk. No single disqualifier could be overcome, and all five apply simultaneously.
all_round2.apply_mechanism: Ashby (Apply for this Job button on JD page)

all_round2.title: Program Manager, Employee Experience
all_round2.h1b: Zero People/HR/EX H1B filings across 2022-2024 (28-37 LCAs per year, all tech/engineering/analytics). No non-tech sponsorship pattern. WARNING: tech-only history + actively downsizing company (23% layoff April 2024).
all_round2.reason: DEAD POSTING. LinkedIn job ID 3747786627 dates to late 2023/early 2024. The role does not appear on Indeed, SimplyHired, or any live aggregator as of June 11, 2026. Chegg conducted a major 23% workforce reduction in April 2024 and has been in sustained decline due to AI disruption to their tutoring business — they are not actively hiring People/EX PMs. Even if the posting were live: (1) Chegg shows zero H1B filings for People/HR/EX roles across three years of LCA data — only tech and a handful of finance/analytics roles were sponsored; (2) this is a Tier 3 relocation scenario (Santa Clara, not Portland/Seattle) requiring SOC-confirmed non-tech sponsorship, which does not exist here; (3) the company's financial trajectory makes any new hire in People Ops unlikely to survive a probation period. Hard PASS on all three grounds: expired posting, no non-tech H1B pattern, company contraction.

all_round2.title: Academic Program Manager
all_round2.h1b: Tech-only H1B history (50 filings, predominantly Staff/Senior Engineers and DevOps). Only non-tech filings: Program Manager (2021, Mountain View), GTM Finance Manager (2022), QA Manager (2021). No People/HR/Learning/Education SOC filings. Per updated rule: WARNING + score penalty, not hard PASS — but role is moot since the posting is not live.
all_round2.reason: POSTING NOT LIVE. The "Academic Program Manager" role was inferred/hypothetical — it does not appear in Guild Education's current job listings. LinkedIn shows 9 active Guild openings (all Senior/Director/Staff level engineering and GTM roles). Guild's careers page (JS-rendered, confirmed via LinkedIn) shows no education, program management, or People roles open. Cannot evaluate a role that does not exist. If this role goes live, re-evaluate: concept fit is P1b (Education Program PM via InGenius bridge), but remote-only position with tech-only H1B history would land around STRETCH 68 with warning flags.

all_round2.title: Director, Talent & Organizational Development
all_round2.h1b: Cap-Exempt (academic health system — H1B cap-exempt by classification, 810 total LCA filings, 1 HR Manager filing identified, but sponsorship at this grade unconfirmed)
all_round2.reason: Hard-reject on two independent grounds: (1) "Director" title is an instant PASS — no consulting-firm exception applies at a hospital/academic health system; (2) Posting is not confirmed live — OHSU's iCIMS external careers board returns zero results for this title, no Google-indexed posting exists, and the Google AI overview identifies Signe Bishop as the current incumbent. Even setting those aside, a Director role at OHSU requires ~7-10 years progressive OD/HR leadership experience vs Jamie's ~3 years — a 4-7 year YOE gap that caps the score well below PASS threshold regardless of cap-exempt status.

all_round2.title: Organizational Development Consultant

However, h1bdata.info shows zero LCA filings for OD/HR/People roles at Seattle Children's — all 2025 filings are clinical, research, and data/technical. No confirmed non-tech People-role sponsorship pattern. Under the updated H1B rule, this is a WARNING + score penalty (not hard PASS alone), but the dead posting overrides everything.
all_round2.reason: DEAD POSTING — hard disqualifier. The direct Seattle Children's careers URL (careers.seattlechildrens.org/us/en/job/R-46820/Organizational-Development-Consultant) returned HTTP 410 Gone, confirming the posting has been removed/expired. No active listing found on LinkedIn search or Indeed. The LinkedIn job ID 3774016592 appears to be a stale cross-post of an already-closed requisition. Do not apply.

all_round2.title: Principal Talent Management Consultant (Hybrid)

all_round2.reason: Two independent hard disqualifiers: (1) Posting is expired — original URL returns 404 on providence.jobs, absent from live Providence job search, LinkedIn, and Indeed. (2) "Principal" title is an explicit hard reject in Jamie's profile (same tier as Director/VP/Senior — implies 5+ years). Even if the posting were live, the title alone would be an instant PASS. all_round2.company: Pacific Northwest University of Health Sciences (PNWU)

all_round2.reason: Hard PASS — explicit "no sponsorship" language in JD is the absolute blocker (overrides all other scoring). Posting is live (May 14, 2026 – Until Filled). Even absent the sponsorship disqualifier, the role requires 3-5 years of higher-ed HR/faculty affairs experience Jamie doesn't have, and is located in Yakima WA (relocation required with no H1B path). Do not apply.

all_round2.title: Student Services Coordinator
all_round2.h1b: Cap-exempt (university). No H1B cap lottery needed — PSU can sponsor directly. Irrelevant here because posting is dead.
all_round2.reason: Posting 49879 is confirmed dead. PSU's ATS returns "This posting is not available" at the exact URL provided, and the role does not appear anywhere in PSU's current 62 open positions. Cannot apply to a closed posting — hard PASS regardless of fit or sponsorship status.

all_round2.title: Coordinator of Basic Needs Center
all_round2.h1b: Cap-exempt university — PSU is on the confirmed cap-exempt list. No "no sponsorship" language in the JD. H1B sponsorship is available, no warning needed.
all_round2.reason: P1c cap-exempt Portland role (strong modifiers: +10 cap-exempt, +5 Portland = 73 before guardrail), but the PRIMARY function is direct social services / trauma-informed case management for food and housing insecure students — not program management or OD. Required: 2 years direct service to low-income populations, crisis management with structural barriers, knowledge of SNAP/TANF/WIC. Jamie has none of this directly. InGenius is academic coaching, not basic needs case work — adjacent but not the same (RULE 0). The ★☆☆ primary-function guardrail caps the score at 65. Additional concern: grant-funded through July 2027 (13-month term). Apply only if Jamie has a genuine interest in basic needs / social services work AND can authentically frame InGenius student support experience — do not stretch the framing to fit.

all_round2.title: Assistant Director of Advising and Student Services
all_round2.h1b: Cap-Exempt 🏛️ — University of Oregon is a public cap-exempt university. h1bdata.info confirms 316 total LCA filings including non-tech advising/student services roles at this grade (Academic Advisor $50K, Interim Student Advisor and Recruiter $51.5K, Campus and Community Engagement Program Associate $52K). Full sponsorship eligibility confirmed.
all_round2.reason: PASS. Cap-exempt Portland role (strong on H1B + location) but core function is university academic advising (1:1, drop-in, group, career counseling) — Jamie has zero university advising experience. Required: 3 years academic advising in a college/university setting + 2 years supervision. Jamie's honest match: ~1 year adjacent (InGenius college prep coaching, not university advising). YOE gap = 2 years below stated minimum → Guardrail 3 caps score at 60. Primary function (academic advising) is ★☆☆ for Jamie → Guardrail 4 also triggers. PM and stakeholder coordination strengths are real but secondary scope here. Hiring manager screen would reject on the 3-year advising credential. Applying burns runway on a low-probability slot.
all_round2.apply_mechanism: University of Oregon careers portal — https://careers.uoregon.edu/en-us/job/536719/ — deadline June 24, 2026

all_round2.title: Program Coordinator, Health Promotion and Wellness Services

all_round2.reason: Strong cap-exempt sponsorship and program coordinator scope (P1c), but core domain is public health/wellness — not OD/People programs. Jamie's background is org psych and talent development, not health education or health promotion. The score-modifiers (cap-exempt +10, P1c +3) are doing heavy lifting; honest role-fit is a near-miss on domain. Worth applying only if Jamie has a warm intro on campus or is genuinely interested in a health-adjacent student services pivot. Without a referral, the domain gap is a real screen-out risk.
all_round2.apply_mechanism: UO Careers portal — upload resume/CV with employment dates, respond to qualification questions, provide 3 professional references (including current/most recent supervisor). Review began May 20; open until filled. URL: https://careers.uoregon.edu/en-us/job/536629/

all_round2.title: Academic Advising Specialist
all_round2.h1b: Cap-exempt institution. OSU has filed H1B petitions specifically for Academic Advisor roles (4 filings confirmed on h1bdata.info), plus other non-tech education/admin roles. Cap-exempt status means no lottery risk — sponsorship is straightforward if hired.
all_round2.location: Corvallis, OR (on-site, limited remote option) — ~85 miles from Portland. In-state but requires relocation or significant commute. Not Portland metro.
all_round2.reason: P1c role (explicitly listed as in-scope: Academic Advising at PNW universities including OSU). OSU is cap-exempt with verified Academic Advisor H1B filings. Posting is live, deadline July 19 2026. Jamie's MS in Applied Org Psych covers student development theory; InGenius Prep provides the student-facing advising bridge (coaching students and teachers, diverse populations, facilitation). Preferred Master's in related field — Org Psych qualifies. GAP: Role is 90% direct 1:1 advising caseload (Banner/Degreeworks, academic warning/probation monitoring) — Jamie has no formal caseload advising experience and no student information system exposure. Her strength is program management and OD consulting, not individual caseload advising. The role leans more toward the '1:1 advising-only' end that profile_compact.md flags as less ideal vs. cohort/program-level student success. Salary $55K-$62K is on the lower end. Score: base 65 (good-adjacent fit, InGenius is real bridge but advising caseload is a gap) + cap-exempt +10 + PNW/Oregon +3 = 78, then -2 for the caseload-primary concern = 76.
all_round2.apply_mechanism: Online application at jobs.oregonstate.edu/postings/180935 — requires resume, cover letter explaining qualifications, and three professional references. Contact: Nicole Kent (nicole.kent@oregonstate.edu).

all_round2.title: Program Manager for Teaching and Learning
all_round2.h1b: Cap-exempt (public research university). H1B LCA data shows 240 filings in 2024, all faculty/research/clinical — zero staff program manager filings in 2023-2024 database. Under updated H1B rule this would be a WARNING with score penalty, not hard PASS. However, posting liveness overrides this analysis.
all_round2.reason: DEAD POSTING — hard disqualifier. Workday API search returns zero results for REQ-0000124810. The posting is no longer live in UW's system (confirmed via direct Workday CXS API call and keyword search both returning 0 matches). No Wayback Machine archive, no aggregator cache found. Could not retrieve JD content from any source. Per evaluation rules, dead/expired postings = instant PASS regardless of fit. Do not apply.

all_round2.title: Program Manager, Deaf and Disability Cultural Center
all_round2.h1b: UW is cap-exempt (flagship state research university). No H1B filings appear in h1bdata.info for Program Manager roles, but cap-exempt employers use separate petition pathways and routinely sponsor international staff. Cap-exempt +10 applies with standard flag. No "no sponsorship" language found in JD. Sponsorship expected: YES.
all_round2.reason: SCORING: Base 70 (P1c — student/community program manager at PNW university, exact tier match) + 10 cap-exempt + 3 P1c + 5 Seattle = 88 raw. Sanity check applied: modifiers (+18) are doing heavy lifting on a 70 base — subtract 10 per guardrail → 78 STRETCH. REAL FIT: Strong alignment on program management ops, community programming, educational initiatives, and stakeholder relationship-building (InGenius student/teacher ops, ODN community org work). GENUINE GAP: This is a culturally specific disability/Deaf student center role. Jamie has zero documented background in disability services, Deaf community, or disability studies — this is the core community the manager must authentically serve and advocate for, not generic program management. That specialization gap is real and will show in interviews. TIER 2 NOTE: Honest match ~72-75%, below the 80% threshold for Seattle Tier 2. Cap-exempt status satisfies Advantage Clause Option A, which allows it to surface. REPOST: REQ-0000124986 posted Oct 2025 (deadline Oct 14) did not fill; REQ-0000128291 is the same role re-posted June 2026. On a local Seattle hybrid, repost-alone = KEEP per rules. LIVE: Confirmed active on Workday as of June 2026 (D Center Instagram and UW Disability Studies confirm the re-post; no expired/closed language found).

No filings found under "talent" or "human resources" keyword searches, but Big4 firms file by generic title — "ASSOCIATE" is the standard title for all entry-level consulting tracks including Talent/Change. all_round2.title: Business Analyst
all_round2.h1b: Confirmed — 227 Business Analyst LCA filings verified on h1bdata.info; McKinsey actively sponsors H1B for this specific title
all_round2.location: Multiple US offices (no Portland); nearest: Seattle, Silicon Valley, San Francisco, Southern California
all_round2.reason: The McKinsey Business Analyst is a flagship general management consulting hire — solving business strategy, operations, finance, and organizational problems across sectors like aerospace, chemicals, oil and gas, and financial services. This is NOT a People/HR/OD consulting role. Jamie's domain is people development and organizational psychology, not business strategy consulting. The core daily work (case-style problem-solving for Fortune 500 strategy and operations) maps to ~35-40% of Jamie's actual experience. Her data analysis and PPT/Excel skills transfer, but the consulting scope is fundamentally misaligned with her career trajectory. Per Guardrail 8: title says 'Business Analyst' but JD body confirms scope has no people-domain qualifier — mandate a JD body read, which confirms the mismatch. Tier 3 relocation requires 85%+ honest match + P1/P1b/P1c scope; this role scores neither. H1B is confirmed (strong) and YOE requirement fits (~3 years, JD says under 4), but sponsorship and YOE alone cannot manufacture a scope fit. Applying would also push Jamie down a general management consulting path that diverges from her HR/OD/talent development career goal.

all_round2.title: Entry Level Analyst – Human Capital / Talent & Organization
all_round2.h1b: HARD PASS — Accenture's official US Employment Statements page states verbatim: "Applicants for employment in the US must have work authorization that does not now or in the future require sponsorship of a visa for employment authorization in the United States." This is explicit company-level no-sponsorship language. Community reports (Fishbowl, recruiter forums) confirm Accenture stopped sponsoring new external H1B hires ~5-6 years ago; current LCA filings (2,904 in FY2025) are almost entirely tech roles (Packaged App Dev, Software Engineering) with zero People/HR/HC analyst sponsorships evidenced. Profile_compact.md lists Accenture as a confirmed sponsor but notes "Accenture T&O = no sponsor" — confirmed here. No SOC 13-1071/13-1151/11-3121 filings found for non-tech roles at Accenture LLP.
all_round2.reason: Hard PASS on sponsorship. Accenture's published US Employment Statements page contains explicit no-sponsorship language for all US applicants. This is a confirmed no-sponsor for external entry-level hires — not a data-lag ambiguity case. The H1B RULE update's "HARD PASS for explicit no-sponsorship JD language" applies here at the company policy level. Role fit itself would be P2 consulting analyst (~72% match: Jamie's OD consulting, data analysis, and stakeholder mgmt map to the HC/T&O analyst scope), but sponsorship is binary and this one fails hard. Do not apply.
